article_items.title: You can no longer Google the word 'disregard' | TechCrunch
article_items.description: After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.
article_items.excerpt: After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.
article_items.summary: More from TechCrunch You can no longer Google the word ‘disregard’ Earlier this week, Google rolled out a completely new Search experience , foregrounding AI summaries and kicking the traditional “10 blue links” far down the page. After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface. The practical impact: But the sheer scale of Google Search means there are lots of edge cases that the company doesn’t seem to have considered. And crucially, the AI response serves no conceivable value to a user searching the word “disregard.” It’s just a broken tool. Watch next: StrictlyVC Athens is up next. After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.

After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.
For instance, this is what you’ll now get if you type the word “disregard” into Google Search.

We tried Google’s AI glasses and they’re almost there Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn’t really Google anymore Google is pitching an AI agent ecosystem to consumers who may not buy it Elon Musk...
I have been a professional tech journalist for nearly 15 years, and before today, I cannot think of a single time when a Bing search result was more valuable than the Google equivalent.
Every weekday and Sunday, you can get the best of TechCrunch’s coverage.
For most users, that single reply is the only thing you’ll see.
But the sheer scale of Google Search means there are lots of edge cases that the company doesn’t seem to have considered.
And crucially, the AI response serves no conceivable value to a user searching the word “disregard.” It’s just a broken tool.
